1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None

7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

1	import argparse
2	import sys
3	
4	from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
5	from .formatter import format_table, format_json
6	
7	
8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()

## Comprehensive Exploration Report: Python CLI Bookmark Management Tool

I have thoroughly explored all source files in this bookmark management CLI tool. Here's the complete analysis:

### 1. PROJECT STRUCTURE

**All Files in Repository:**
- `/pyproject.toml` - Project configuration
- `/src/cli.py` - CLI entry point and command definitions
- `/src/models.py` - Bookmark data model
- `/src/storage.py` - Persistence layer (JSON file storage)
- `/src/formatter.py` - Output formatting (table and JSON)
- `/src/__init__.py` - Package initialization (empty)
- `/tests/test_storage.py` - Storage functionality tests
- `/tests/test_search.py` - Search functionality tests (defines expected behavior)
- `/tests/__init__.py` - Test package initialization (empty)

The project is minimal and well-organized with clear separation of concerns.

---

### 2. DATA MODEL FOR BOOKMARKS

**Bookmark Class** (`src/models.py`):
```python
@dataclass
class Bookmark:
    id: str                    # UUID generated on creation
    url: str                   # Full URL of the bookmark
    title: str                 # Display title
    tags: List[str]           # List of zero or more tag strings
    notes: str                # Optional additional notes (default: "")
    created_at: str           # ISO format timestamp (auto-generated)
```

**Key Methods:**
- `to_dict()` - Converts bookmark to dictionary for JSON serialization
- `from_dict()` - Class method to create Bookmark from dictionary (with defaults for optional fields)

---

### 3. MAIN CLI ENTRY POINT & COMMAND DEFINITIONS

**CLI Module** (`src/cli.py`):

**Entry Point:** `main(argv=None)` function, configured in pyproject.toml as the "bookmark" command

**Command Parser Structure:**
- Uses `argparse.ArgumentParser`
- Subcommands defined via `add_subparsers(dest="command")`
- No command defaults to printing help and exiting with code 1

**Implemented Commands:**

1. **`add`** - Add a new bookmark
   - Required: `url` (positional)
   - Required: `title` (positional)
   - Optional: `--tags` (nargs="*", default=[])
   - Optional: `--notes` (default="")
   - Output: Prints bookmark ID

2. **`list`** - List all bookmarks
   - Optional: `--format` (choices: "table", "json", default: "table")
   - Output: Formatted bookmarks

3. **`delete`** - Delete a bookmark
   - Required: `id` (positional)
   - Output: Success/not found message
   - Exit code: 1 if not found

4. **`show`** - Display a single bookmark
   - Required: `id` (positional)
   - Output: Bookmark in table format
   - Exit code: 1 if not found

---

### 4. STORAGE & PERSISTENCE LAYER

**Storage Module** (`src/storage.py`):

**Database Location:** `~/.bookmarks.json` (configurable via `db_path` parameter)

**Core Functions:**

- **`load_bookmarks(db_path)`** - Loads all bookmarks from JSON file
  - Returns empty list if file doesn't exist
  - Returns list of Bookmark objects

- **`save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)`** - Persists bookmarks to JSON
  - Writes indented JSON (2-space indent)
  - Creates file if it doesn't exist

- **`add_bookmark(url, title, tags=None, notes="", db_path)`** - Creates and persists new bookmark
  - Generates UUID for id
  - Sets created_at to current ISO timestamp
  - Returns the created Bookmark object

- **`delete_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)`** - Removes bookmark by ID
  - Returns True if found and deleted, False otherwise

- **`get_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)`** - Retrieves single bookmark by ID
  - Returns Bookmark object or None

---

### 5. OUTPUT FORMATTING SYSTEM

**Formatter Module** (`src/formatter.py`):

- **`format_table(bookmarks)`** - Plain-text table format
  - Column layout: ID (8 chars) | Title (30 chars) | URL (40 chars) | Tags (20 chars)
  - Truncates values to column widths with padding
  - Returns "No bookmarks found." for empty lists
  - Includes header and separator line

- **`format_json(bookmarks)`** - JSON output
  - Calls `b.to_dict()` on each bookmark
  - Uses `json.dumps()` with 2-space indent
  - Outputs complete bookmark data including all fields

---

### 6. TEST STRUCTURE & TESTING PATTERNS

**test_storage.py** - Tests for persistence layer
- **Fixtures:**
  - `db_path` - Temporary file path for each test
  - `sample_bookmarks` - Pre-populated Bookmark objects

- **Test Classes:**
  - `TestLoadBookmarks` - 2 tests (missing file, loading)
  - `TestSaveBookmarks` - 2 tests (file creation, JSON validity)
  - `TestAddBookmark` - 3 tests (creation, persistence, unique IDs)
  - `TestDeleteBookmark` - 2 tests (deletion, missing ID handling)
  - `TestGetBookmark` - 2 tests (retrieval, missing ID handling)

**test_search.py** - Defines expected search functionality (module not yet implemented)
- **Fixtures:**
  - `bookmarks` - 5 sample bookmarks with various tags and domains

- **Expected Functions to Implement:**
  - `search_by_title(bookmarks, query)` - Case-insensitive substring match
  - `search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag)` - Exact tag match
  - `search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain)` - Domain matching (including subdomains)
  - `search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None)` - Combined search with AND logic

- **Test Classes:**
  - `TestSearchByTitle` - 3 tests (case insensitive, substring, no match)
  - `TestSearchByTag` - 3 tests (exact match, shared tags, no match)
  - `TestSearchByDomain` - 3 tests (domain match, subdomain match, no match)
  - `TestSearchCombined` - 7 tests (single criterion, AND logic, conflicting criteria)
  - `TestSearchCLI` - 2 tests (CLI parser integration, format flag)

**Testing Framework:** pytest with temporary file fixtures

---

### 7. EXISTING FILTERING/SEARCH FUNCTIONALITY

**Currently Implemented:**
- None. The CLI only supports basic CRUD operations.

**Planned (via test_search.py):**
The test file defines a comprehensive search system that needs to be implemented:
- **search_by_title()** - Searches bookmark titles (case-insensitive substring)
- **search_by_tag()** - Filters by tag name (exact match)
- **search_by_domain()** - Filters by domain/subdomain from URL
- **search()** - Combined search supporting all three criteria with AND logic
- **CLI integration** - `search` command with `--title`, `--tag`, `--domain`, and `--format` flags

---

### 8. REUSABLE UTILITY FUNCTIONS

**Available Utilities:**

1. **Bookmark Creation & Conversion:**
   - `Bookmark.to_dict()` - Serialize to dict
   - `Bookmark.from_dict(data)` - Deserialize from dict

2. **Storage Operations:**
   - `load_bookmarks(db_path)` - Read all bookmarks
   - `save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)` - Write all bookmarks
   - Pattern: Load → Modify → Save (used by add/delete operations)

3. **Formatting:**
   - `format_table(bookmarks)` - Print-friendly table
   - `format_json(bookmarks)` - JSON serialization
   - Both handle empty lists gracefully

4. **UUID Generation:**
   - Used in `add_bookmark()` via `uuid.uuid4()`

**Reusability Notes:**
- All storage functions accept `db_path` parameter, allowing multiple database instances
- Bookmark model is a standard dataclass, easily serializable
- Formatter functions work with any List[Bookmark]
- Search operations (when implemented) should follow the same pattern

---

### SUMMARY

This is a clean, well-structured CLI tool with:
- **4 implemented commands**: add, list, delete, show
- **Dual output formats**: table and JSON
- **Persistent JSON storage**: ~/.bookmarks.json
- **Rich bookmark data model**: id, url, title, tags, notes, created_at
- **Comprehensive test suite**: 12 existing tests + 12 planned search tests
- **Clear architecture**: Models → Storage → Formatter → CLI

The tool is ready for enhancement with the search functionality defined in the test suite.

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

These tests define the expected behaviour of the search module that needs

from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title

class TestSearchByTitle:

        assert "RunPod Python SDK" in titles

    def test_substring_match(self, bookmarks):

        assert result[0].id == "eee-555"

        parser = build_parser()
        args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--tag", "docs", "--format", "json"])
        assert args.command == "search"
        assert args.tag == "docs"
        assert args.format == "json"

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:

    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:

    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

# Plan: Add `search` Command to Bookmark CLI

## Context

The bookmark CLI tool (src/cli.py) supports add, list, delete, and show commands but has no way to filter bookmarks. `tests/test_search.py` already defines the full expected behavior — it imports from `src.search` (which doesn't exist yet) and tests both the search logic and CLI integration. The task is to implement exactly what the tests expect, so all tests pass.

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| File | Action |
|---|---|
| `src/search.py` | **Create** — new search module |
| `src/cli.py` | **Modify** — add `search` subcommand |

---

## Step 1 — Create `src/search.py`

Implement four functions. Use `urllib.parse.urlparse` (stdlib) to extract the netloc for domain matching.

```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import List, Optional
from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on title."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against any element in b.tags."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Match bookmarks whose URL netloc equals domain (handles subdomains)."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Combined AND search. No criteria → returns all bookmarks."""
    result = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        result = search_by_title(result, title)
    if tag is not None:
        result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
    return result
```

**Key decisions:**
- Domain match is exact netloc equality (`urlparse(url).netloc == domain`). This satisfies both `test_matches_domain` (query="github.com" matches "github.com") and `test_matches_subdomain` (query="docs.python.org" matches "docs.python.org").
- AND logic is implemented by chaining: each filter narrows the previous result.
- `search()` with no criteria passes `bookmarks` through unchanged, satisfying `test_no_criteria_returns_all`.

---

## Step 2 — Modify `src/cli.py`

### 2a. Add import at top
```python
from .search import search
```

### 2b. Register the subcommand in `build_parser()`

Add after the `show` subparser block:

```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Filter by title substring")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Filter by exact tag")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Filter by domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

### 2c. Handle the command in `main()`

Add an `elif` branch after the `show` handler:

```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

No new exit codes needed — empty results are handled gracefully by `format_table` ("No bookmarks found.") and `format_json` ("[]").

---

## Verification

Run the full test suite:
```bash
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

Expected: all tests in `tests/test_search.py` and `tests/test_storage.py` pass.

Spot-check the CLI manually (optional):
```bash
bookmark search --title python
bookmark search --tag docs --format json
bookmark search --title python --tag sdk --domain github.com
```

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains query (case-insensitive substring match)."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have the given tag (exact match)."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL netloc exactly equals domain."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Filter bookmarks using AND logic across any combination of criteria.

    If no criteria are provided, all bookmarks are returned.
    """
    result = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        result = search_by_title(result, title)
    if tag is not None:
        result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
    return result

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    # --- search ---
    search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
    search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Filter by title substring (case-insensitive)")
    search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Filter by tag (exact match)")
    search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Filter by domain (exact netloc match)")
    search_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
